obligations.reason: Debug interface can access CSRs; if it bypasses privilege checks without proper authentication, sensitive machine-mode state could be altered by an external debugger.
obligations.subject: external debugger through debug_req_i
obligations.object: machine-mode CSRs (e.g., mstatus, mepc, mcause, mtvec, mie, debug CSRs dcsr, dpc)
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization state (e.g., debug unlock) before granting CSR write access, or privilege-level check even in debug mode for certain CSRs
obligations.candidate_path: debug_req_i → debug_mode_q → CSR write path in csr_regfile (csr_we, csr_wdata) without privilege check
state_conditions_to_check: absence of debug authentication signal being checked before CSR write
whether csr_we is qualified by current privilege level when in debug mode
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug authentication or limited access
observed behavior: write succeeds without authentication
path: from debug_req_i to csr_we generation in csr_regfile
state condition: debug_mode_q active, auth state missing
impact: unauthorized modification of processor security state
source locations: csr_regfile.sv signals debug_mode_q, csr_we, CSR address decode
obligations.uncertainty: No debug authentication signal (e.g., debug unlock) visible in fact layer; need to verify if one exists and is used

obligations.reason: User-mode code should not be able to directly write to machine-mode CSRs; a missing privilege check would allow privilege escalation.
obligations.subject: CPU instruction with CSR write (e.g., CSRRW) executed in user mode (umode_i == 1)
obligations.object: machine-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mie, etc.) and supervisor-mode CSRs if in user mode
obligations.expected_guard: privilege-level check: current PRIV_LVL_U must raise illegal instruction exception and prevent write
obligations.candidate_path: instruction decode → csr_we generation in csr_regfile → write to CSR registers without checking PRIV_LVL_U
state_conditions_to_check: umode_i == 1 or current privilege level is U
csr_we is asserted for machine-mode CSR address
read_access_exception or similar exception not generated
required_evidence_slots: observed behavior: write proceeds or exception missing
path: fu_op/csr_addr_i to csr_we and exception logic
impact: user code can gain machine-mode control
source locations: csr_regfile.sv around csr_we, read_access_exception, privilege check
obligations.uncertainty: Privilege check logic for CSR writes not completely visible; need to examine csr_regfile implementation

obligations.reason: Debug interface may read CSRs containing sensitive information (e.g., exception PC, trap values) without authentication, leaking state.
obligations.subject: external debugger through debug_req_i
obligations.object: sensitive CSRs (mepc, mcause, mtval, sepc, stval, dpc, debug scratch registers)
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication before granting read access to non-debug CSRs, or restriction that debug mode cannot read certain CSRs without proper privilege
obligations.candidate_path: debug_req_i → debug CSR read mux (csr_rdata_o) → output to debugger without authentication check
state_conditions_to_check: absence of auth check on CSR read path
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: authentication or access policy
path: from CSR read mux to csr_rdata_o
impact: information disclosure of processor state
source locations: csr_regfile.sv CSR read logic
obligations.uncertainty: It is unclear whether RISC-V debug spec permits unrestricted CSR reads; need to verify desired security policy

obligations.reason: The TSR, TW, TVM bits in mstatus enforce hypervisor-like S-mode restrictions; if they can be cleared by lower privilege modes or bypassed by debug, supervisor isolation is broken.
obligations.subject: supervisor-mode code or debug interface
obligations.expected_guard: only machine mode (or trusted firmware) may clear these bits; debug should not bypass without authentication
obligations.candidate_path: CSR write to mstatus in csr_regfile without checking current privilege mode, or debug path directly writing to these fields
state_conditions_to_check: csr_we to mstatus and write to TSR/TW/TVM fields
required_evidence_slots: path: csr_we and address decode to mstatus, bit-level update
state condition: privilege or debug mode
impact: S-mode control over hypervisor protections
source locations: csr_regfile.sv mstatus write logic, privilege check
obligations.uncertainty: Need to confirm if TSR/TW/TVM are write-protected after being set; dependency on mstatus field update mechanism

obligations.reason: Performance monitor counters (cycle, instret, etc.) and their enable bits (mcounteren/scounteren) may leak timing information or allow covert channels if user mode can read them without appropriate enable.
obligations.subject: user-mode software
obligations.object: performance counters (cycle, instret) and delegated CSRs (cycle/instret read-only aliases)
obligations.expected_guard: mcounteren/scounteren bits must be set to allow user-mode read; otherwise illegal instruction exception
obligations.candidate_path: csr_addr_i decode for cycle/instret CSRs, read mux, and exception logic when mcounteren bit is zero
state_conditions_to_check: csr_rdata_o returns counter value and no exception raised
required_evidence_slots: observed behavior: read succeeds without exception
path: CSR read logic, counteren check
impact: information leakage of system activity
source locations: csr_regfile.sv counter code, read_access_exception generation
obligations.uncertainty: Not all counter enable logic visible; need to inspect mcounteren/scounteren implementation

obligations.reason: After reset, critical security state (debug enable, privilege level, lock bits) must be safe; a debug-req immediately after reset could hijack boot if debug mode is not correctly gated.
obligations.subject: external debugger asserting debug_req_i shortly after reset
obligations.expected_guard: debug mode should be disabled until a secure boot sequence explicitly enables it, or an authentication handshake is completed
obligations.candidate_path: debug_req_i → debug_mode_q (and set_debug_pc_o) without a 'debug authorized' state or lifecycle state check
state_conditions_to_check: debug_mode_q set to 1 before any secure software lock
no debug unlock or lifecycle fuse check present
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: debug disable/lock until authorized
observed behavior: debug mode becomes active immediately
state condition: reset just completed, no firmware lock
impact: bypass secure boot and gain full control
obligations.uncertainty: No lifecycle state or debug authorization signal visible; core may rely on external pin/permanent disable which is not in this scope
